entries.title: hello — minimal Console.println
entries.summary: Smallest-possible program: import stdio, inject the system handler, print one line. The canonical entry point for new readers.

entries.title: fib — recursive integer fibonacci
entries.summary: Classic recursion benchmark; demonstrates simple integer arithmetic and Console output.

entries.title: math — i64 arithmetic helpers
entries.summary: Walks through abs / max / min / pow style helpers and prints the results.

entries.title: module_test — import and reuse a stdlib symbol
entries.summary: Shows the multi-line `import { ... }` shape and the round-trip through `std:str.from_i64`.

entries.title: di_cap — user-defined Logger cap + console handler
entries.summary: Demonstrates the dependency-injection pattern: declare a `cap`, write a console-backed handler, install it with `inject`, and route business-logic calls through it.

entries.title: network_access — live HTTP GET via the Net cap
entries.summary: Fires a real network request through `Net.get` and prints the response body. Demonstrates `PermNet` and `try/catch` for transient failures.

entries.title: bench_parallel — sequential vs lazy.force_all walltime
entries.summary: Side-by-side comparison of running N CPU-bound tasks one-at-a-time versus dispatching them across WASI threads with `lazy.force_all`.

entries.title: handler_basic — define a cap, install a handler, perform
entries.summary: User-defined Greeter cap with two interchangeable handlers (polite / shouty) routed by nested `inject` blocks. Handlers are direct calls — no continuation capture.

entries.title: effects_console_fs — two caps used together
entries.summary: A single function requires both Console and Fs; the program writes / reads / removes a tempfile and catches FsThrow group failures.

entries.title: linear_consume — %x is consumed exactly once
entries.summary: Linear `%Box` parameter says 'this function consumes the value'; pattern destructure + ctor rebuild is the in-place 'modify' idiom.

entries.title: linear_borrow — read a linear value via & without consuming
entries.summary: `&Counter` borrows; the linear handle is still alive after the call and can be consumed later. Mirrors hashmap's `get(&map)` vs `put(%map)` split.

entries.title: match_basics — four canonical match shapes
entries.summary: Literal, record destructure, list head-tail, and ctor binding — plus the `_` wildcard. The starter set every reader should know.

entries.title: match_guards_or — guards and or-patterns
entries.summary: `when <bool>` guards on arms and `|`-separated or-patterns for matching alternative shapes in a single arm.

entries.title: match_nested — sum-of-product destructure
entries.summary: Pattern-match on a constructor whose payload is itself a constructor; demonstrates deep destructure binding.

entries.title: generics_poly_fn — `fn <T>(...)` parametric functions
entries.summary: User-defined polymorphic function uniform over T, instantiated at different concrete types in the call sites.

entries.title: list_basics — literals, head/tail, std:list combinators
entries.summary: Build lists with `[a, b, c]` and `x :: xs`, match with `[] | head :: tail`, traverse with `list.map / length / reverse`.

entries.title: record_destructure — pattern-bind record fields
entries.summary: Single-arm record destructure inside a `let` or `match`, including the punned `{ field }` short-form.

entries.title: mutable_ref — heap cell update via ref
entries.summary: Demonstrates the small mutable-reference surface used by stateful handlers (assign + dereference).

entries.title: lazy_force — @t suspends until first read
entries.summary: Single-thunk lazy binding; the RHS is not evaluated until `@t` is read. Forms the basis of `lazy.force_all` parallelism.

entries.title: lazy_parallel — force_all dispatches @ thunks across threads
entries.summary: `lazy.force_all([t0,t1,...])` runs each thunk on its own WASI thread and joins results in input order. Thunks must be independent (no shared mutable state).

entries.title: try_catch — recover from a raised exception
entries.summary: Wrap a fallible call in `try / catch | Exn -> ... end`; demonstrates the `throws { ... }` row and the catch-arm pattern shape.

entries.title: exception_group — closed sums via `exception group`
entries.summary: Declares a closed sum of exceptions with `exception group G = A | B` and matches each arm individually in catch.

entries.title: option_basics — Some / None as a typed null replacement
entries.summary: Build with `Some(val: ...)` / `None`, inspect via match or `unwrap_or`, chain via `map`. The 'may be absent' contract is encoded in the function signature.

entries.title: result_basics — Ok / Err for effect-free errors
entries.summary: Use `Result<T, E>` when the caller should decide how to recover. Compose with `map`, `and_then`, `unwrap_or`. Alternative to `throws { ... }`.

entries.title: math_basics — std:math integer + float helpers
entries.summary: abs / max / min / sqrt / floor / ceil plus the `i64_to_float` / `float_to_i64` bridge. Demonstrates the i64-vs-float split.

entries.title: str_basics — std:str byte / char operations
entries.summary: length / contains / index_of / starts_with / to_lower / trim plus `from_i64` / `from_float` / `from_bool` for primitive→string.

entries.title: hashmap_basics — linear i64→i64 map
entries.summary: `std:hashmap` handle is linear: writes consume + return, reads borrow (`&HashMap`), end with `free`. `Lookup = Found | Missing` avoids sentinel values.

entries.title: stringmap_basics — linear string→i64 map
entries.summary: Sister of hashmap with string keys. Same linear handle discipline (put consumes, get borrows). Useful for symbol tables / config.

entries.title: char_basics — code-point classification
entries.summary: `std:char.is_alpha / is_digit / is_upper / is_ident_*` plus `digit_value` / `hex_digit_value` (return -1 for non-digits).

entries.title: tuple_pair — Pair<A,B> stand-in for tuples
entries.summary: Nexus has no anonymous tuples; `std:tuple` exports `Pair(left, right)` plus `fst` / `snd`. Use for two-value returns without naming a domain type.

entries.title: rand_basics — Random as a capability
entries.summary: `Random` cap with `next_i64`, `range`, `next_bool`. Use `system_handler` for production, custom handlers for deterministic fixtures. `seed_with` reproduces sequences.

entries.title: clock_basics — wall-clock time as a capability
entries.summary: `Clock.now` returns millis since epoch; `Clock.sleep` cooperatively blocks. Both gated by `PermClock` so pure code cannot observe time.

entries.title: env_basics — process environment via Env cap
entries.summary: `Env.get(key)` returns `Option<string>`; `Env.set` writes to the process table. Permission-gated by `PermEnv`.

entries.title: log_basics — structured logging via the Logger cap
entries.summary: Logger cap with trace/debug/info/warn/error arms. `console_logger` emits text, `json_logger` emits JSON-Lines. `set_min_level` filters below the threshold.

entries.title: proc_argv — read program argv via Proc
entries.summary: `Proc.argv` returns `[ string ]` (excluding the binary path). `Proc.exit` halts. `Proc.exec` (not shown) shells out and bundles stdout/stderr/exit-code.

entries.title: network_request — Net cap and Header builder
entries.summary: Demonstrates the request shape (header() helper, [Header] list) without firing a live call. See examples/network_access.nx for a real fetch.

entries.title: json_basics — parse / serialize JsonValue
entries.summary: `std:json` defines JsonValue as a sum of JsonNull / JsonBool / JsonInt / JsonFloat / JsonString / JsonArray / JsonObject. parse raises JsonThrow on bad input.

entries.title: bytebuffer_basics — linear ByteBuffer accumulator
entries.summary: `std:bytebuffer` is a linear handle: push_* consumes + returns, length/get_byte/to_string borrow, end with free. Used by codegen/json/serialization paths.

entries.title: regexp_basics — Thompson NFA compile + find + gsub
entries.summary: from_string compiles (raising InvalidRegexp); test/find/find_all do matching; gsub does global replace. Match carries start/end/text + group captures.

entries.title: pbt_basics — property-based testing via std:pbt
entries.summary: `pbt.forall(g, prop, n)` draws n samples from generator g and shrinks the first counterexample greedily. small_int / bool_gen / string_gen are bundled.

entries.title: exn_todo — typed placeholder via exn.todo()
entries.summary: `exn.todo()` is Nexus' answer to Rust `todo!()`: a typed placeholder that compiles anywhere but raises `NotImplemented` at runtime. Each call site adds to the throws row.

entries.title: let_destructure — irrefutable pattern bind in let
entries.summary: `let Ctor(field: x, ...) = expr` binds fields directly; short for a one-arm match. The compiler still checks irrefutability.

entries.title: import_forms — the three shapes of `import`
entries.summary: Named (`{ ... }`), wildcard alias (`* as m`), and combined (`{ ... }, * as m`). Conventions: named for short surfaces, wildcard for frequent/ambiguous use.

entries.title: if_expression — if is a value-producing expression
entries.summary: `if ... then ... else ... end` evaluates to a value; both branches must agree on type. Use inline for two-way; reach for `match` when ≥3 outcomes.

entries.title: recursive_loop — tail-recursion replaces for/while
entries.summary: Nexus has no for/while. Idiomatic loop is a tail-recursive accumulator helper; codegen lowers to wasm return_call so deep iteration is constant-stack.

entries.title: linear_unused — %x leaves scope unconsumed
entries.summary: A linear binding must be consumed exactly once. Letting it fall out of scope is a compile-time error.

entries.title: lazy_unforced — @t leaves scope unforced
entries.summary: An @-binding suspends a computation that must be observed once. Leaving the thunk unforced is rejected.

entries.title: lazy_double_force — @t observed twice
entries.summary: Lazy bindings are at-most-once. A second `@t` read is a static error.

entries.title: main_wrong_return — main must return unit
entries.summary: The entry point's return type is required to be `unit`. Any other type trips the dedicated MainNotUnit arm.

entries.title: non_exhaustive_match — sum type missing a variant arm
entries.summary: match must cover every constructor of the scrutinee. Omitting one (without a wildcard) is a typecheck error.

entries.title: type_mismatch — string assigned to i64 binding
entries.summary: A `let _: T = e` annotation must agree with the RHS type. String into i64 fails.

entries.title: unbound_variable — referenced identifier not in scope
entries.summary: Variables must be in scope at the reference site. Typos and missing imports both surface this diagnostic.

entries.title: missing_argument — call omits a named parameter
entries.summary: Every named parameter must be supplied. The compiler does not silently default.

entries.title: parse_unterminated_string — string literal without closing quote
entries.summary: Double-quoted strings must close on the same line. The parser reports the failure at the next statement boundary.

entries.title: import_module_not_found — std:NAME has no corresponding file
entries.summary: `import "std:NAME"` resolves to `nxlib/stdlib/NAME.nx`. The loader reports the missing file.

entries.title: option_unwrap_none — unwrap(None) raises RuntimeError
entries.summary: `std:option.unwrap` is partial: it raises `RuntimeError` on `None`. Compiles cleanly; surfaces as a non-zero exit at run time.
